query: Status of Russia State Duma bill No. 1194918-8 'On Digital Currency and Digital Rights' second and third reading July 2026. Has the second reading occurred? When is third reading scheduled? Any news from July 2026 about the schedule.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

**Direct answers to your questions:**

1. **Has the second reading occurred?** As of July 13, 2026: **No — not the plenary (full-chamber) second reading.** Only the State Duma's Committee on Financial Markets (chaired by Anatoly Aksakov) had approved the amended bill text *for* second reading, reported ~July 8, 2026. This committee clearance is distinct from the actual plenary vote, which remained pending.

2. **When is the third reading scheduled?** The **second and third readings were tentatively scheduled together for July 21, 2026** (Russian practice often holds them on the same/consecutive days). No source confirmed they had actually happened yet.

3. **July 2026 schedule news:** The bill missed its original July 1, 2026 adoption deadline due to "protracted approvals with government agencies" and multiple amendments. The law is now targeted to **enter into force September 1, 2026**, pending the Duma readings, Federation Council approval, and President Putin's signature. Bank of Russia implementing regulations are expected by ~November 2026, with the first legal regulated crypto transactions in early 2027.

**Notable amendments in the second-reading version:** removal of the wallet-address disclosure requirement, a 300,000-ruble annual cap for non-qualified investors, easing of some domestic payment/swap restrictions, and a 48-hour delay on large cross-border transfers.

sources.title: Russia to enforce new crypto law on September 1
sources.snippet: Second & Third Reading tentatively scheduled for July 21, 2026; law to enter force September 1, 2026; original deadline was July 1, 2026; delay attributed by Aksakov to protracted approvals with government agencies.

sources.title: Russia's amended crypto bill to permit wider use of coins in payments
sources.snippet: Draft law 'On Digital Currency and Digital Rights' approved for second reading by the Committee on Financial Markets; still requires Federation Council and Putin's signature; September 1 enforcement deadline.

sources.title: Russia Advances Crypto Oversight Bill With Easier Reporting Rules
sources.snippet: July 8, 2026: Financial Markets Committee approved revised bill for second reading; dropped wallet address disclosure; 300,000 ruble investor cap; no plenary reading dates specified.

sources.title: Russia advances crypto bill after dropping wallet address disclosure rule
sources.snippet: State Duma committee approved revised bill for second reading, removing wallet address declaration requirement; per Aksakov's Telegram channel.

sources.title: Russia Advances Crypto Regulation Bill to Legalize Foreign Trade Settlements
sources.snippet: Bill 1194918-8 advanced in first reading; recognizes crypto as property; for foreign trade not domestic payments; unlicensed platforms banned July 2027; originally expected to take effect July 1, 2026.

sources.title: Russia Advances Sweeping Crypto Regulation Bill
sources.snippet: Lawmakers approved the draft in first reading with 327 of 340 deputies voting in favor; introduced by the Russian government.

sources.title: Russia expects delay in crypto market regulation bill
sources.snippet: Ministry of Finance expects a short delay in adoption; final adoption requires further parliamentary readings and presidential approval, rollout expected later in the year.

sources.title: Russia advanced crypto regulation bill to allow limited digital asset payments and swaps
sources.snippet: Financial Markets Committee approved updated draft 'On Digital Currency and Digital Rights' for its second reading; would legalize certain coin-to-coin exchanges and permit crypto payments in specific situations.

data_points.metric: Tentative date for second and third readings
Expected law entry into force date
Annual crypto investment cap for non-qualified investors per intermediary
